mnemonics.title: ER Diagram Components
mnemonics.content: ERD = R E D: Rectangle = Entity, Ellipse = Attribute, Diamond = Relationship

mnemonics.title: Attribute Types
mnemonics.content: S.C.D.M.S: Simple, Composite, Derived, Multi-valued, Single-valued - remember as 'Smart Computers Don't Make Mistakes'

mnemonics.title: Cardinality Types
mnemonics.content: 1:1 = One to One, 1:N = One to Many, M:N = Many to Many - Think 'N for Many'

mnemonics.title: Weak Entity Identification
mnemonics.content: W.E.A.K = Weak Entity Always Needs owner Key (partial key + owner PK)

mnemonics.title: Participation Types
mnemonics.content: T.P.P = Total Participation = Participation (must have), Partial = optional (may have)

mnemonics.title: Key Hierarchy
mnemonics.content: S.C.P.F: Super key → Candidate key → Primary key → Foreign key

acronyms.usage: ER Model, ER Diagram, ER Modeling - conceptual design of databases
Unique identifier for each record in a table, cannot be NULL
Attribute that references Primary Key of another table, creates relationships
Potential Primary Key - attributes that can uniquely identify entities
Set of attributes that can uniquely identify entities, may contain extra attributes
General term for relationship involving N entities (binary=2, ternary=3, quaternary=4)
Used to represent associative entities in ER diagrams

visualTips.visualization: Think of entities as NOUNS in a sentence. If you can count it as a 'thing' (Student, Book, Order), it's an entity. Rectangle box reminds you it's a concrete object.
Attributes are ADJECTIVES describing entities. Student has Name, Age, GPA - these describe the Student. Draw as bubbles attached to the entity rectangle.
Relationships are VERBS connecting entities. Student ENROLLS in Course, Employee WORKS IN Department. Draw as diamonds (action shapes).
Weak entity has a 'ghost' appearance - double rectangle. Like a dependent who can't exist without the employee (owner). Think of it as financially dependent.
Crow's foot looks like a bird's foot pointing at 'many'. The line (|) means 'one'. Together they show relationship strength. More points = more entities related.
Double line = total (forced participation - MUST have). Single line = partial (optional - MAY have). Think double line as 'required' like double the requirement.

analogies.analogy: Entity is like a noun in English - a person, place, or thing that exists and can be uniquely identified. Like 'Student', 'Bank', 'Product'.
Weak entity is like a child - cannot exist independently without a parent. A Dependent is like a child who needs an Employee (parent) to exist in the system.
Primary Key is like your fingerprint - unique to you, never changes, and uniquely identifies you among all people.
Foreign Key is like a referral - it points to something else. Employee's DeptID refers to Department table's DeptID, creating a link.
Relationship is like a marriage - connects two entities with specific rules (cardinality) about how many can be involved.
Multi-valued attribute is like having multiple phone numbers. One person can have several, unlike single-valued like gender.
Composite attribute is like a address - made of multiple parts (street, city, zip) but we treat it as one address unit.
Derived attribute is like a receipt total - you could calculate it from individual items, but you store it for convenience.
